title: Making a two-volume slipcase
sources: Douglas Cockerell, Bookbinding and the Care of Books (1901) — slipcase geometry described. Arthur Johnson, The Thames and Hudson Manual of Bookbinding (1978, copyright, structural description only).
notes: A drop-spine slipcase (the book goes in from the top) sized to hold two matching volumes side by side. Covers: measuring the books precisely (all five faces: fore-edge, head, tail, spine, depth), calculating the five board pieces (two sides, back, top, base), cutting sequence (always cut grain parallel to the spine), gluing and reinforcing the joints, covering in book cloth with mitred corners, optional ribbon pull. Distinguish from a solander box (which has a hinged lid): a slipcase is open at the top only.
